title: limonium vulgare subsp. behen
intro: Limonium vulgare subsp. behen, also known as sea lavender or sea pinks, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which make it beneficial for treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Limonium vulgare subsp. behen include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of sea lavender can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to calm digestive issues, reduce anxiety, and promote a restful sleep.
